examples: 'route_desc': ' The Beaver Creek Express operates between 6:30 am to 10:00 pm' -> 'The Beaver Creek Express operates between 6:30 am to 10:00 pm'
fixes.label: Trimmed surrounding whitespace

findings.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
findings.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

findings.effort: A rules file; one rule can cover a flat fare across the network.
findings.fix: Add fare_leg_rules.txt linking each fare product to the trips it applies to (by network, area, or time).
findings.what: The feed publishes fare products but no fare_leg_rules saying which trips they apply to.
findings.why: Trip planners can't attach a price to a trip, so riders still see no fare even though products are published.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (100% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Trip distance exceeds shape distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Enable 'remove unused shapes' (or similar) in your export tool.
findings.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 3 kinds of issue across 64 instances (0 error, 64 warning, 0 informational).

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 145 days.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: Service data covers the next 151 days.
States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 100% of trips.

conformance.summary: This feed earns the conformance mark: valid, current, and stating wheelchair access on nearly every stop and trip.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: If you report to the National Transit Database, your GTFS agency_id should be your five-digit NTD ID by report year 2026 (2025 for full reporters). We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this one is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data covers the next 151 days.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed looks ready for your NTD certification: published at a public URL, valid, and current. The official check is still your D-10 certification.

recommendations.effort: Usually a short file naming the discount groups you already publish.
recommendations.fix: Add rider_categories.txt for the fares you already offer, then reference those categories from your fare products.
recommendations.what: The feed publishes fare products but no rider_categories.txt defining who qualifies for each fare.
recommendations.why: Without rider categories, apps can only show the full adult fare and can't surface senior, youth, or student discounts a rider may be eligible for.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 1 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (Blue).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 5 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("West Beaver Creek Blvd", "Chapel Square/Mtn Family Health", "Avon Crossing/ Canyon Run", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

top_fixes.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
top_fixes.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
top_fixes.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.
